---
title: "Beyond the Chat Box: AI at Your Desk"
subtitle: "Audience Notes — Getting Started with Claude Cowork"
author: "Dr. Logan Kelly | Small Business AI Center"
date: "TBD 2026"
format:
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    geometry:
      - margin=0.75in
    fontsize: 10pt
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{booktabs}
        \usepackage{xcolor}
        \definecolor{uwrfred}{HTML}{BE0F34}
        \definecolor{uwrfblue}{HTML}{2EC7FF}
        \definecolor{promptbg}{HTML}{F5F5F5}
        \usepackage{enumitem}
        \setlist{nosep, leftmargin=1.2em}
        \usepackage{titlesec}
        \titleformat{\section}{\large\bfseries\color{uwrfred}}{}{0em}{}[\vspace{-0.3em}\rule{\textwidth}{0.5pt}]
        \titleformat{\subsection}{\normalsize\bfseries}{}{0em}{}
        \titleformat{\subsubsection}{\small\bfseries\itshape}{}{0em}{}
        \titlespacing*{\section}{0pt}{1.2em}{0.4em}
        \titlespacing*{\subsection}{0pt}{0.8em}{0.2em}
        \titlespacing*{\subsubsection}{0pt}{0.5em}{0.2em}
        \usepackage{tcolorbox}
        \tcbuselibrary{breakable}
        \newtcolorbox{promptbox}[1][]{
          colback=promptbg,
          colframe=uwrfred,
          fonttitle=\small\bfseries,
          boxrule=0.5pt,
          arc=2pt,
          left=6pt, right=6pt, top=4pt, bottom=4pt,
          breakable,
          title=#1
        }
        \newtcolorbox{followupbox}[1][]{
          colback=white,
          colframe=uwrfblue,
          fonttitle=\small\bfseries,
          boxrule=0.5pt,
          arc=2pt,
          left=6pt, right=6pt, top=4pt, bottom=4pt,
          breakable,
          title=#1
        }
        \usepackage{fancyhdr}
        \pagestyle{fancy}
        \fancyhf{}
        \fancyfoot[C]{\small\color{gray} Small Business AI Center}
        \fancyfoot[R]{\small\color{gray}\thepage}
        \renewcommand{\headrulewidth}{0pt}
---

\begin{center}
{\small Small Business AI Center}
\end{center}

\section{What is Claude Cowork?}

Cowork is Anthropic's desktop AI mode where Claude works \emph{with the files
on your computer} instead of inside a chat tab. You point it at a folder,
describe a result, and Cowork plans, executes, and saves real files back to
that folder.

\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{Reads your folders} --- no more copy-paste, no more re-upload
\item \textbf{Writes real files} --- \texttt{.docx}, \texttt{.xlsx}, \texttt{.pdf} you can open and edit
\item \textbf{Runs skills} --- reusable mini-programs for repeating workflows
\item \textbf{Uses connectors} --- Slack, Gmail, QuickBooks, Calendar, more
\item \textbf{Schedules tasks} --- daily briefings or weekly reports automatically
\item \textbf{Stays local} --- runs in an isolated VM; files don't leave your machine
\end{itemize}

\subsection{Cowork vs. Chat --- when to use which}

\begin{tabular}{@{}p{0.45\textwidth}p{0.45\textwidth}@{}}
\toprule
\textbf{Chat} & \textbf{Cowork} \\
\midrule
One file at a time, re-uploaded & A folder Cowork can read and write \\
Text in a chat window & Real files saved back \\
Re-type the prompt each time & Skills, scheduled tasks, plugins \\
Files travel to the cloud & Runs in an isolated VM locally \\
Good for quick Q\&A & Good for multi-step work in your files \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}

\section{Three Steps to Start}

\subsection{Step 1 --- Install Claude Desktop}

\textbf{Download:} \texttt{claude.com/download} --- pick macOS or Windows.

\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{Mac:} Apple Silicon (M1 or newer) required for Cowork
\item \textbf{Windows:} Pro / Enterprise / Education (Hyper-V required); not Home
\item \textbf{Plan:} Pro (\$20/mo), Max, Team, or Enterprise --- not free tier
\item \textbf{Install:} Admin rights needed on first install
\end{itemize}

\subsection{Step 2 --- Run /setup-cowork}

In your first Cowork session, type \texttt{/setup-cowork} and press enter.
Guided setup walks you through:

\begin{itemize}
\item Pick your role (small biz owner, lawyer, marketer, etc.)
\item Install a plugin matched to that role
\item Connect your tools --- Slack, Google or Microsoft 365, QuickBooks, etc.
\item Try a skill --- your first repeatable workflow
\end{itemize}

\subsection{Step 3 --- Pick a Folder}

Click \textbf{``Work in a folder''} in the chat bar. Cowork reads context from
that folder and saves finished work back to it. You can change folders any
time --- it's just a permission scope.

\textbf{Tip:} make a fresh folder for each project. Cowork sees everything in it.

\section{Demo 1: What's in My Folder?}

\textbf{Setup:} point Cowork at a folder of business documents.

\begin{promptbox}[Prompt]
Read everything in this folder. Then tell me:

\begin{enumerate}
\item What's here --- a short index of files with one-line descriptions
\item What needs my attention this week --- anything time-sensitive,
      decisions owed, or follow-ups overdue
\item What's not here that probably should be --- gaps you'd ask me about
\end{enumerate}

Don't write any files yet --- just report back.
\end{promptbox}

\begin{followupbox}[Follow-up (if useful)]
For the items you flagged as needing attention this week, draft a short
reply or response for each one. Save the drafts as a single Word document
in this folder called \texttt{Drafts\_For\_Review.docx}.
\end{followupbox}

\section{Demo 2: Saving Work as a Skill}

\textbf{Setup:} after Demo 1, in the same Cowork session.

\begin{promptbox}[Prompt]
Save what we just did as a skill called ``Monday Briefing.'' It should:

\begin{itemize}
\item Read the current working folder
\item Produce the same 3-part report
\item Run once per week --- set it up as a Monday morning scheduled task
\end{itemize}

Tell me what you've saved and how I trigger it next week.
\end{promptbox}

\begin{followupbox}[Follow-up (if useful)]
What other workflows in my week look like good candidates to turn into
skills? Don't build them --- just suggest 3 or 4 based on what you've seen.
\end{followupbox}

\section{Three Things to Try This Week}

\begin{enumerate}
\item \textbf{Install Claude Desktop} and run \texttt{/setup-cowork} once.
      Even if you don't use it daily --- the install itself takes ~10 minutes.
\item \textbf{Make a ``Cowork sandbox'' folder.} Pick a low-stakes folder
      (last quarter's invoices, an old project) and let Cowork read it. Get
      a feel for what it sees.
\item \textbf{Save one prompt you re-type as a skill.} The first time you
      find yourself pasting the same instructions twice, stop and turn it
      into a skill.
\end{enumerate}

\section{Privacy \& Trust}

\begin{itemize}
\item Cowork runs in an \textbf{isolated virtual machine} on your computer
\item Files never leave your machine for training
\item Network access is allowlisted
\item You see what Cowork plans before it acts --- approve, edit, or cancel each step
\end{itemize}

\section{Contact}

\begin{tabular}{@{}ll@{}}
\textbf{Logan Kelly, Ph.D.} & \\[0.3em]
\textit{Codirector, Small Business AI Center} & \textit{Professor of Economics} \\
Universities of Wisconsin & School of Business and Economics \\
Office of Business \& Entrepreneurship & University of Wisconsin--River Falls \\
Phone: 715-425-3335 & Phone: 715-425-3335 \\
Email: logan.kelly@business.wisconsin.edu & Email: logan.kelly@uwrf.edu \\
\end{tabular}

\section{Resources}

\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{Download:} \texttt{claude.com/download}
\item \textbf{Get started guide:} \texttt{support.claude.com} $\rightarrow$ ``Get started with Claude Cowork''
\item \textbf{Three-step intro:} \texttt{claude.com/resources/tutorials/get-started-in-claude-cowork-in-three-steps}
\item \textbf{Small business tutorial:} \texttt{claude.com/resources/tutorials/using-claude-for-your-small-business}
\end{itemize}
